verbatim_text: So, good morning, everybody. Thank you so much for coming to today's colloquium talk. We have our guest today, Valerie Chen, visiting us from CMU in the machine learning department, where Valerie is advised by Amit Kawalka. Valerie's work is on human AI collaboration, how to measure AI's ability to work well with humans, and how to improve all of that, which is quite different from a lot of work we see these days on improving the capabilities of AI. For all of this work that Valerie has been doing, she's won several awards, including the Rising Stars in Data Science, the CMU Presidential Fellowship, and Best Paper Award at a New York's workshop. With that introduction, please join me in welcoming Valerie. Okay, very important. I must turn on my mic. Okay. Can everyone hear me, including in the back? Perfect. Okay, so hopefully I'm not screaming and you're feeling a little loud. Okay, so thanks for the great introduction. Very excited to be here and also looking forward to more conversations later and also into the rest of the day.
